is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of children with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria treated with AL or SP in Tanzania.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfcrt K76 (codon 76) and pfmdr1 N86 (codon 86) frequencies in Table 1.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives numerator/denominator: pfcrt K76 (26+23)/102=0.480 at D0; pfmdr1 N86 (27+30)/102=0.559 at D0, etc.

sub_country_location.evidence: Study conducted in Fukayosi, Bagamoyo District, Tanzania — a named sub-national site below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Samples collected during April–July 2004, a single-year window well within 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria enrolled in Fukayosi, Tanzania. (Table 2 in vitro lab lines excluded.)

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study analyzing samples from a clinical efficacy trial plus in vitro assays.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples derive from a previously reported clinical trial in Fukayosi, Tanzania [ref 2, Martensson et al.], and the authors reference earlier related analyses of the same cohort; the baseline/recurrent samples originate from that trial.

supplement.evidence: Extractable per-locus frequencies with a named sub-country site and collection window are all in the main text and Table 1.

assessment.summary: Primary study analyzing pfcrt K76 and pfmdr1 N86 (and other) SNP frequencies in P. falciparum from children in a 2004 AL/SP efficacy trial in Fukayosi, Bagamoyo District, Tanzania. Table 1 provides extractable numerator/denominator frequencies at baseline and in recurrent infections, tied to a sub-country site and a single-year collection window. Table 2 lab-strain in vitro data are excluded from field-sample consideration. Meets all criteria, though samples come from a previously reported trial cohort (duplicate risk).
